info.title: Airborne
info.description: Service for managing OTA updates and configurations

post.description: Register a file by URL, recording its metadata and assigning it a version. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Use this when the file is already hosted somewhere the server can reach; to upload the bytes directly, use UploadFile instead. Requires a bearer token.

get.description: List files grouped by path, so that all versions and tags of a file appear together. Supports pagination and optional search and tag filters. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search query to filter files by path

schema.description: Search query to filter files by path

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Tags to filter files by (comma-separated for multiple values)

schema.description: Tags to filter files by (comma-separated for multiple values)

get.description: List files for an application, with pagination and optional search and tag filters. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Tags to filter files by (comma-separated for multiple values, e.g., "prod,dev,staging")

schema.description: Tags to filter files by (comma-separated for multiple values, e.g., "prod,dev,staging")

post.description: Upload a file's bytes directly as the request body and register it in one step. Send the raw file as the payload, with its Base64-encoded SHA-256 digest in the x-checksum header and the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Returns the created file. Requires a bearer token.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path where the file will be stored on sdk

schema.description: Path where the file will be stored on sdk

parameters.name: x-checksum
parameters.description: SHA-256 digest of the file, encoded in Base64, used by the server to verify the integrity of the uploaded file

schema.description: SHA-256 digest of the file, encoded in Base64, used by the server to verify the integrity of the uploaded file

patch.description: Update the tag of an existing file, identified by its file key in the path (a file path with a version or tag, e.g. "path/to/file@version:3" or "path/to/file@tag:prod"). Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Returns the updated file. Requires a bearer token.

parameters.name: file_key
parameters.description: The file key in the path (e.g., "$file_path@version:$version_number" or "$file_path@tag:$tag")

schema.description: The file key in the path (e.g., "$file_path@version:$version_number" or "$file_path@tag:$tag")

get.description: List all organisations the authenticated user belongs to, along with their applications and the caller's access level in each. Requires a bearer token.

post.description: Create a new application inside an organisation. Pass the target organisation in the x-organisation header. Returns the created application and the caller's access levels. Requires a bearer token.

Create a targeting dimension (standard or cohort) that releases can be targeted against. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

get.description: List the targeting dimensions defined for an application, in priority order, with pagination. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

delete.description: Delete a dimension by name. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

put.description: Update a dimension, identified by name in the path — for example to change its priority position. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

post.description: Create a new organisation owned by the authenticated user. Returns the created organisation with its (initially empty) application list and the caller's access levels. Requires a bearer token.

Submit a request to have an organisation provisioned (for onboarding flows that require manual approval), including contact details and store links. Returns a confirmation message. Requires a bearer token.

Create a package: an index file plus the set of files that make up an OTA bundle. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Returns the created package with its assigned version. Requires a bearer token.

get.description: List packages for an application, with pagination and optional search by index file path. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term for filtering packages using index file path

schema.description: Search term for filtering packages using index file path

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: If true, fetch all packages without pagination

schema.description: If true, fetch all packages without pagination

post.description: Create a new release. A release points a package (and any resources) at a set of targeting dimensions; ramp it later to roll it out. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Returns the created release with its resolved config and package. Requires a bearer token.

get.description: List releases for an application, with pagination and optional filtering by status or targeting dimension. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Count of releases per page for pagination (default: 50)

schema.description: Count of releases per page for pagination (default: 50)

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: If true, fetch all releases without pagination

schema.description: If true, fetch all releases without pagination

parameters.name: x-dimension
parameters.description: dimension to filter releases in format key1=value1;key2=value2

schema.description: dimension to filter releases in format key1=value1;key2=value2

get.description: Get a single release by its id, including its config, package, resources, targeting dimensions, and experiment details. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

List the signing keys of an application, with the default key first. Every application
is provisioned with a default key when it is created, so this is never empty. The
private key is never returned — only the public key, which is enough to verify a
signed release config. Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and
x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

post.description:
Create a signing key for an application. The server generates an ECDSA P-256 keypair
and keeps the private half; the response carries only the public key. The new key is
not used until it is made the default (see SetDefaultSigningKey) or named explicitly
in the x-signing-key-id header — except for the very first key an application has,
which becomes its default automatically. Key IDs must be unique within the
application; a duplicate ID is rejected with a 409. IDs may contain only lowercase
letters, digits, and single dashes; a dash cannot be first, last, or repeated. Pass
the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers.
Requires a bearer token.

patch.description:
Enable or disable a signing key. A disabled key signs nothing, and naming it in
x-signing-key-id is rejected. The application's default key cannot be disabled — it is
what signs every release config served without an explicit key — so promote another key
to default first (see SetDefaultSigningKey). Pass the organisation and application in
the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires a bearer token.

post.description:
Make a signing key the application's default: from then on it signs every release
config served without an explicit x-signing-key-id header. Exactly one key per
application is the default, so the previous default is demoted in the same operation.
A disabled key cannot be made the default — enable it first. This is how a key is
rotated: create a new key, distribute its public key to your clients, then promote it.
Pass the organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers.
Requires a bearer token.

get.description:
Download a signing key's public key as a PEM document (SPKI, "application/x-pem-file",
served as an attachment). Distribute this to whatever verifies the x-airborne-signature
header on a served release config. The private key is never downloadable. Pass the
organisation and application in the x-organisation and x-application headers. Requires
a bearer token.

post.description: Exchange user credentials (client_id and client_secret) for an access token and a refresh token. Public — no auth token required. Call this first, then send the returned access token as a bearer token on subsequent requests.

get.description: Get the authenticated user's profile, including the organisations they belong to and the caller's access level in each. Requires a bearer token.

Version 2 of the release-resolution endpoint: resolves and returns the active release configuration for an application based on the caller's targeting dimensions. This is the endpoint newer SDKs call at boot. Public — no auth token required.

The response is signed: the x-airborne-signature response header carries an ES256 signature over the exact raw JSON response body bytes, so a client can verify the config was served by Airborne and not tampered with in transit. Send the x-signing-key-id header to choose which key signs the response; omit it to use the application's default key. See the "Signing keys" endpoints for managing keys and downloading the public key to verify against.

parameters.name: x-signing-key-id
parameters.description:
ID of the signing key to sign the response with. This is the readable ID chosen
when the key was created. Optional — when omitted, or sent with an empty value,
the application's default signing key is used. A non-empty key ID that is
invalid, unknown, disabled, or belongs to another application is rejected with
a 400.

schema.description:
ID of the signing key to sign the response with. This is the readable ID chosen
when the key was created. Optional — when omitted, or sent with an empty value,
the application's default signing key is used. A non-empty key ID that is
invalid, unknown, disabled, or belongs to another application is rejected with
a 400.

x-airborne-signature.description:
Signature over the response body, in the form
keyid="<key-id>",alg="es256",sig="<base64>".

The signature is ECDSA P-256 over SHA-256 (ES256), DER-encoded and then Base64-encoded,
computed over the exact raw JSON response body bytes, uncompressed. Verify it with the
public key of the signing key named in keyid, downloadable via GetSigningKeyPublicKey.

Absent if the application has no usable signing key.

schema.description:
Signature over the response body, in the form
keyid="<key-id>",alg="es256",sig="<base64>".

The signature is ECDSA P-256 over SHA-256 (ES256), DER-encoded and then Base64-encoded,
computed over the exact raw JSON response body bytes, uncompressed. Verify it with the
public key of the signing key named in keyid, downloadable via GetSigningKeyPublicKey.

Absent if the application has no usable signing key.

get.description:
Resolve and return the active release configuration for an application, given the caller's targeting dimensions. This is the endpoint the SDK calls at boot. Public — no auth token required.

The response is signed: the x-airborne-signature response header carries an ES256 signature over the exact raw JSON response body bytes, so a client can verify the config was served by Airborne and not tampered with in transit. Send the x-signing-key-id header to choose which key signs the response; omit it to use the application's default key. See the "Signing keys" endpoints for managing keys and downloading the public key to verify against.

parameters.name: x-signing-key-id
parameters.description:
ID of the signing key to sign the response with. This is the readable ID chosen
when the key was created. Optional — when omitted, or sent with an empty value,
the application's default signing key is used. A non-empty key ID that is
invalid, unknown, disabled, or belongs to another application is rejected with
a 400.

schema.description:
ID of the signing key to sign the response with. This is the readable ID chosen
when the key was created. Optional — when omitted, or sent with an empty value,
the application's default signing key is used. A non-empty key ID that is
invalid, unknown, disabled, or belongs to another application is rejected with
a 400.

x-airborne-signature.description:
Signature over the response body, in the form
keyid="<key-id>",alg="es256",sig="<base64>".

The signature is ECDSA P-256 over SHA-256 (ES256), DER-encoded and then Base64-encoded,
computed over the exact raw JSON response body bytes, uncompressed. Verify it with the
public key of the signing key named in keyid, downloadable via GetSigningKeyPublicKey.

Absent if the application has no usable signing key.

schema.description:
Signature over the response body, in the form
keyid="<key-id>",alg="es256",sig="<base64>".

The signature is ECDSA P-256 over SHA-256 (ES256), DER-encoded and then Base64-encoded,
computed over the exact raw JSON response body bytes, uncompressed. Verify it with the
public key of the signing key named in keyid, downloadable via GetSigningKeyPublicKey.

Absent if the application has no usable signing key.

tenant_info.description: Tenant-specific configuration, as a JSON document.

ConflictErrorResponseContent.description: Conflict error, returned when a request conflicts with the current state of the resource — for example, creating something whose name is already taken.

access.description: Access levels of the user for the organisation

depends_on.description: Identifier of the dimension this depends on (required for cohort dimensions, ignored for standard dimensions)

file_path.description: Path where the file will be stored on sdk

url.description: URL from where the file can be downloaded

metadata.description: Metadata associated with the file in Stringified JSON format or a file attachment

file_path.description: Path where the file is stored on sdk

url.description: URL from where the file can be downloaded

size.description: Size of the file in bytes

created_at.description: Date of creation of the file

file_path.description: Path where the file is stored on sdk

url.description: URL from where the file can be downloaded

size.description: Size of the file in bytes

created_at.description: Date of creation of the file

applications.description: List of applications under the organisation

access.description: Access levels of the user for the organisation

tag.description: Optional tag to identify the package.

files.description: Space Separated file ids to be included in the package

version.description: Version number assigned to the package.

index.description: File id of the package's index (entry) file.

files.description: File ids included in the package.

release_config_timeout.description: Timeout for the release config in seconds

boot_timeout.description: Timeout for the package in seconds

properties.description: Properties of the config in Stringified JSON format

CreateReleaseRequestContent.description: Request body for creating a release.

properties.description: Properties of the package in Stringified JSON format or a file attachment

CreateSigningKeyRequestContent.description: Request body and headers for creating a signing key.

key_id.description:
ID for the new key. Must be unique within the application. It may contain only
lowercase letters (a-z), digits (0-9), and single dashes, and cannot start or end
with a dash.

CreateSigningKeyResponseContent.description:
A signing key used to sign the release config an application serves to its SDKs.

The private key never leaves the server: it is not part of this shape and is not
returned by any endpoint. Only the public key is exposed, so that clients can verify
the x-airborne-signature header on a served release config.

key_id.description:
Key ID chosen when the key is created. This is the value clients send in the
x-signing-key-id header to have a release config signed with this key, and the
value that appears in the keyid field of the x-airborne-signature response header.

algorithm.description: Algorithm the key signs with. Always "ecdsa-p256" today.

public_key.description:
Public half of the keypair, PEM-encoded (SPKI). Use this to verify signatures
produced with this key.

is_default.description:
Whether this is the application's default key. Exactly one key per application is
the default: it signs every release config served without an explicit
x-signing-key-id header.

disabled.description:
Whether the key is disabled. A disabled key signs nothing and is rejected if it is
named in x-signing-key-id. The default key cannot be disabled.

mandatory.description: Whether a value for this dimension is required when targeting.

DimensionsMap.description: Map of dimension name to value.

FileGroup.description: Represents a group of file versions

file_path.description: The file path (unique identifier for the group)

total_versions.description: Total number of versions for this file

tags.description: List of tags associated with versions

FileGroupTag.description: Represents a tag associated with a specific version

version.description: The version this tag is associated with

FileGroupVersion.description: Represents a version within a file group

url.description: URL from where the file can be downloaded

size.description: Size of the file in bytes

created_at.description: Date when this version was created

GetReleaseConfig.description: Resolved release configuration returned to callers.

release_config_timeout.description: Time allowed for fetching the release config, in seconds.

boot_timeout.description: Time allowed for the app to boot, in seconds.

GetReleaseResponse.description: A release with its full details.

resources.description: Additional resources served with the release.

GetReleaseResponseContent.description: A release with its full details.

resources.description: Additional resources served with the release.

GetSigningKeyPublicKeyOutputPayload.description: The public half of the keypair, PEM-encoded (SPKI), as the raw response body.

organisations.description: Organisations the user belongs to, with the user's access level in each.

total_items.description: Total number of groups matching the query

ListSigningKeysResponseContent.description: An application's signing keys, default first.

applications.description: List of applications under the organisation

PostLoginResponseContent.description: Tokens returned after a successful login.

ReleaseExperiment.description: Details of the experiment backing a release, used to ramp it out gradually.

package_version.description: Package version served by the experiment.

config_version.description: Config version served by the experiment.

traffic_percentage.description: Percentage of traffic currently routed to this release.

message.description: Message indicating the status of the request

ServeFile.description: A file as served to the SDK, with the location and checksum needed to download and verify it.

url.description: URL the SDK downloads the file from.

checksum.description: Checksum used to verify the downloaded file.

ServePackage.description: A package as served to the SDK: the index file plus the files that make up the OTA bundle.

properties.description: Package properties, as a JSON document.

important.description: Files that must be downloaded before boot.

lazy.description: Files that can be downloaded lazily after boot.

resources.description: Additional resources for the release, as a JSON document.
Additional resources for the release, as a JSON document.

SetDefaultSigningKeyResponseContent.description:
A signing key used to sign the release config an application serves to its SDKs.

The private key never leaves the server: it is not part of this shape and is not
returned by any endpoint. Only the public key is exposed, so that clients can verify
the x-airborne-signature header on a served release config.

key_id.description:
Key ID chosen when the key is created. This is the value clients send in the
x-signing-key-id header to have a release config signed with this key, and the
value that appears in the keyid field of the x-airborne-signature response header.

algorithm.description: Algorithm the key signs with. Always "ecdsa-p256" today.

public_key.description:
Public half of the keypair, PEM-encoded (SPKI). Use this to verify signatures
produced with this key.

is_default.description:
Whether this is the application's default key. Exactly one key per application is
the default: it signs every release config served without an explicit
x-signing-key-id header.

disabled.description:
Whether the key is disabled. A disabled key signs nothing and is rejected if it is
named in x-signing-key-id. The default key cannot be disabled.

SigningKey.description:
A signing key used to sign the release config an application serves to its SDKs.

The private key never leaves the server: it is not part of this shape and is not
returned by any endpoint. Only the public key is exposed, so that clients can verify
the x-airborne-signature header on a served release config.

Key ID chosen when the key is created. This is the value clients send in the
x-signing-key-id header to have a release config signed with this key, and the

algorithm.description: Algorithm the key signs with. Always "ecdsa-p256" today.
